previous_headings: I want to contribute > Reporting bugs
title: Contributing to {toro}
text: good bug report shouldn’t leave others needing chase information. Therefore, ask investigate carefully, collect information describe issue detail report. Please complete following steps advance help us fix potential bugs fast possible. Make sure using latest version. Determine bug really bug error side e.g. using incompatible environment components/versions (Make sure read documentation. looking support, might want check section). see users experienced (potentially already solved) issue , check already bug report existing bug error bug tracker. Also make sure search internet (including Stack Overflow) see users outside GitHub community discussed issue. Stack trace (Traceback) OS, Platform Version (Windows, Linux, macOS, x86, ARM) Version interpreter, compiler, SDK, runtime environment, package manager, depending seems relevant. Possibly input output Can reliably reproduce issue? can also reproduce older versions?

what: Create an app.R file for testing
title: Contributing to {toro}
text: Inside inst/examples/, create new file named app.R add following code : file already included .gitignore allow test changes Shiny app. test outside Shiny app simply run: devtools::load_all() load recent changes local repository. map() test map function.
code: library(shiny) devtools::load_all("../") # Load the most recent changes from the local repository  ui <- fluidPage(   toro::mapOutput("map") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$map <- toro::renderMap({     toro::map()   }) }  if (interactive()) {   shinyApp(ui, server) }

title: Animations
text: following examples ’ll use line representing route around Aotearoa.
code: library(sf) #> Linking to GEOS 3.13.0, GDAL 3.8.5, PROJ 9.5.1; sf_use_s2() is TRUE library(toro)  nz_line <- sf::st_sf(   id = 1,   geometry = sf::st_sfc(     sf::st_linestring(       cbind(         c(           172.2041,           182.2494,           168.1286,           163.9383,           171.7449         ),         c(           -32.56960,           -38.85522,           -48.49620,           -46.43999,           -34.62533         )       )     ),     crs = 4326   ) )

title: Animations
text: basic example, add route (line) use add_animation_controls() add controls map animating along route.
code: map() |>   add_route(route_id = "nz_route", points = nz_line) |>   add_animation_controls(route_id = "nz_route")

title: Animations
text: can add customisable speed control. default, 3 speed options: 0.5x, 1x, 2x. can change speed options passing custom values labels add_animation_controls() function.
code: map() |>   add_route(route_id = "nz_route", points = nz_line) |>   add_animation_controls(     route_id = "nz_route",     include_speed_control = TRUE,     speed_values = c(0.3, 0.5, 1, 2, 4),     speed_labels = c("Slower", "Slow", "Default", "Fast", "Faster")   )

title: Animations
text: can also supply icon animated along route. default, toro built image ID “toro-pin” can use purpose, can add custom image. use custom image, use add_image() add image pass settings.animatingIcon parameter add_route() specify icon want animate along route.
code: map() |>   add_route(     route_id = "nz_route",     points = nz_line,     settings = c(list(       animatingIcon = get_layout_options(         "symbol",         list(icon_image = "toro-pin", icon_size = 0.5)       )     ))   ) |>   add_animation_controls(route_id = "nz_route")

title: Animations
text: can set dropVisited TRUE animation settings leave marker map point visited animation. can also change styles different parts route using animatedRouteStyle, unanimatedRouteStyle, visitedRouteStyle settings.
code: map() |>   add_route(     route_id = "nz_route",     points = nz_line,     settings = c(list(       dropVisited = TRUE,       routeLine = list(         "line-color" = "#11a366",         "line-width" = 2,         "line-dasharray" = c(2, 4)       ),       visitedPoints = list(         "circle-color" = "#20de60",         "circle-radius" = 4       ),       animatingPoint = list(         "circle-color" = "#dbe811",         "circle-radius" = 8       )     ))   ) |>   add_animation_controls(route_id = "nz_route")

title: Draw controls
text: Draw modes can set passing modes parameter. default draw mode polygon. also line, point, delete modes can add. polygon draw mode shape finished close polygon clicking dot point started . line draw mode shape finished click last draw point. Using delete mode can delete drawn shapes map. delete shape, first click shape want delete click delete button. using delete mode, drawn shapes static. means editable clicking shape trigger event update maps clicked feature input. using delete mode, drawn shapes editable double clicked (deletable via delete button). keep track shapes updated via edits, can watch <mapId>_shape_updated input. input ID behaves way create shape event except triggered user finished editing existing shape (click outside shape longer edit mode).
code: library(toro)  map() |>   add_draw_control(modes=c("polygon", "line", "delete"))

title: Draw controls
code: library(shiny) library(toro)  ui <- fluidPage(   toro::mapOutput("map"),   fluidRow(     column(       6,       tagList(         h2("Most Recently Created Shape"),         verbatimTextOutput("created_shape")       )     ),     column(       6,       tagList(         h2("Most Recently Updated Shape"),         verbatimTextOutput("updated_shape")       )     )   ),   h2("Most Recently Clicked Shape"),   actionButton("delete_shape", "Delete clicked shape"),   verbatimTextOutput("clicked_shape") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$map <- toro::renderMap({     toro::map() |>       add_draw_control(modes = c("polygon", "line", "point", "delete"))   })    output$created_shape <- renderPrint({     req(input$map_loaded)     get_drawn_shape(input$map_shape_created)   })    output$updated_shape <- renderPrint({     req(input$map_loaded)     get_drawn_shape(input$map_shape_updated)   })    output$clicked_shape <- renderPrint({     req(input$map_loaded)     get_clicked_feature(input$map_feature_click)   })    observe({     req(input$map_loaded)     req(input$map_feature_click)     req(input$map_feature_click$properties$id)     delete_drawn_shape(       mapProxy("map"),       input$map_feature_click$properties$id     )   }) |>     bindEvent(input$delete_shape) }  if (interactive()) {   shinyApp(ui, server) }

title: Draw controls
text: Customise colour drawn shapes using active_colour inactive_colour parameters. labels draw buttons can also changed using mode_labels parameter.
code: library(toro)  map() |>   add_draw_control(     modes = c("polygon", "delete"),     active_colour = "#20de60",     inactive_colour = "#11A366",     mode_labels = list(polygon = "Draw Polygon", delete = "Delete shape")   )

title: All examples
text: Sources required add data map.

title: Add a circle layer
text: First, set base map data.
code: library(toro) library(sf) #> Linking to GEOS 3.13.0, GDAL 3.8.5, PROJ 9.5.1; sf_use_s2() is TRUE library(dplyr) #>  #> Attaching package: 'dplyr' #> The following objects are masked from 'package:stats': #>  #>     filter, lag #> The following objects are masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union library(spData) #> To access larger datasets in this package, install the spDataLarge #> package with: `install.packages('spDataLarge', #> repos='https://nowosad.github.io/drat/', type='source')`  elevation_data <- spData::nz_height |>   sf::st_transform(4326) |>   dplyr::mutate(     info = paste("Elevation:", format(elevation, big.mark = ","))   )

title: Add a circle layer
code: map() |>   set_bounds(elevation_data, padding = 100) |>   add_circle_layer(     id = "elevation",     source = elevation_data,     hover_column = "info"   )

title: Add a circle layer
code: map() |>   set_bounds(elevation_data, padding = 100) |>   add_circle_layer(     id = "elevation",     source = elevation_data,     hover_column = "info",     paint = get_paint_options(       "circle",       options = list(         colour = get_column_steps(           "elevation",           c(3000, 3500),           c("#093d4a", "#11a366", "#dbe811")         ),         circle_radius = get_column_steps(           "elevation",           c(3000, 3500),           c(2, 3, 4)         )       )     ),     layout = get_layout_options(       "circle",       options = list(         "circle-sort-key" = get_column_steps(           "elevation",           c(3000, 3500),           c(1, 2, 3)         )       )     )   )

title: Add a fill layer
text: First, set base map data.
code: library(toro) library(spData) #> To access larger datasets in this package, install the spDataLarge #> package with: `install.packages('spDataLarge', #> repos='https://nowosad.github.io/drat/', type='source')` library(dplyr) #>  #> Attaching package: 'dplyr' #> The following objects are masked from 'package:stats': #>  #>     filter, lag #> The following objects are masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union library(sf) #> Linking to GEOS 3.13.0, GDAL 3.8.5, PROJ 9.5.1; sf_use_s2() is TRUE   region_data <- spData::nz |>   dplyr::rename(geometry = geom) |>   sf::st_transform(4326) |>   dplyr::mutate(     popup = paste("Population:", format(Population, big.mark = ","))   )

title: Add a fill layer
code: map() |>   set_bounds(region_data, padding = 100) |>   add_fill_layer(     id = "regions",     source = region_data,     hover_column = "Name",     popup_column = "popup"   )

title: Add a fill layer
code: map() |>   set_bounds(region_data, padding = 100) |>   add_fill_layer(     id = "regions",     source = region_data,     hover_column = "Name",     popup_column = "popup",     paint = get_paint_options(       "fill",       options = list(         colour = get_column_steps(           "Population",           c(50000, 500000, 1000000),           c("#093d4a", "#11a366", "#20de60", "#dbe811")         ),         opacity = 0.7       )     )   )

title: Add a line layer
text: First, set base map data.
code: library(toro) library(spData) #> To access larger datasets in this package, install the spDataLarge #> package with: `install.packages('spDataLarge', #> repos='https://nowosad.github.io/drat/', type='source')` library(sf) #> Linking to GEOS 3.13.0, GDAL 3.8.5, PROJ 9.5.1; sf_use_s2() is TRUE  seine_data <- spData::seine |>   sf::st_transform(4326)

title: Add a line layer
code: map() |>   set_bounds(seine_data, padding = 100) |>   add_line_layer(     id = "seine",     source = seine_data,     hover_column = "name"   )

title: Add a line layer
code: map() |>   set_bounds(seine_data, padding = 100) |>   add_line_layer(     id = "seine",     source = seine_data,     hover_column = "name",     paint = get_paint_options(       "line",       options = list(         colour = get_column_group(           "name",           c("Marne" = "#014f86", "Seine" = "#61a5c2"),           "#a9d6e5"         ),         line_width = get_column_group("name", c("Seine" = 3), 1)       )     )   )

title: Add a symbol layer
text: First, set base map data.
code: library(toro) library(sf) #> Linking to GEOS 3.13.0, GDAL 3.8.5, PROJ 9.5.1; sf_use_s2() is TRUE library(dplyr) #>  #> Attaching package: 'dplyr' #> The following objects are masked from 'package:stats': #>  #>     filter, lag #> The following objects are masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union library(spData) #> To access larger datasets in this package, install the spDataLarge #> package with: `install.packages('spDataLarge', #> repos='https://nowosad.github.io/drat/', type='source')`  elevation_data <- spData::nz_height |>   sf::st_transform(4326) |>   dplyr::mutate(     info = paste("Elevation:", format(elevation, big.mark = ","))   )

title: Add a symbol layer
code: map() |>   set_bounds(elevation_data, padding = 100) |>   add_symbol_layer(     id = "elevation",     source = elevation_data,     hover_column = "info",     can_cluster = FALSE   )

title: Add a text layer
text: First, set base map data.
code: library(toro) library(sf) #> Linking to GEOS 3.13.0, GDAL 3.8.5, PROJ 9.5.1; sf_use_s2() is TRUE library(dplyr) #>  #> Attaching package: 'dplyr' #> The following objects are masked from 'package:stats': #>  #>     filter, lag #> The following objects are masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union library(spData) #> To access larger datasets in this package, install the spDataLarge #> package with: `install.packages('spDataLarge', #> repos='https://nowosad.github.io/drat/', type='source')`  elevation_data <- spData::nz_height |>   sf::st_transform(4326) |>   dplyr::mutate(     info = paste("Elevation:", format(elevation, big.mark = ","))   )

title: Add a text layer
code: map() |>   set_bounds(elevation_data, padding = 100) |>   add_text_layer(     id = "elevation",     source = elevation_data,     layout = get_layout_options(       "text",       options = list(         text_field = toro::get_column("elevation")       )     )   )

title: Add a text layer
code: map() |>   set_bounds(elevation_data, padding = 100) |>   add_text_layer(     id = "elevation",     source = elevation_data,     layout = get_layout_options(       "text",       options = list(         text_field = toro::get_column("elevation"),         text_size = get_column_steps(           "elevation",           c(3000, 3500),           c(14, 20, 26)         ),         "symbol-sort-key" = get_column_steps(           "elevation",           c(3000, 3500),           c(1, 2, 3)         )       )     ),     paint = get_paint_options(       "text",       options = list(         colour = get_column_steps(           "elevation",           c(3000, 3500),           c("#093d4a", "#11a366", "#04aac1")         )       )     )   )

previous_headings: Adding a latitude and longitude grid layer
title: Latitude and longitude grid layer
code: library(toro) map() |>   add_lat_lng_grid("red")

previous_headings: Adding a latitude and longitude grid layer
title: Latitude and longitude grid layer
text: info controls see Controls Vignette
code: library(toro) map() |>   add_lat_lng_grid("red") |>   add_visibility_toggle("lat_lng_grid")

title: Add a feature service source
text: ArcGIS Feature Service documentation

what: What is a feature service source?
title: Add a feature service source
text: Feature Service web service provides access geographic features attributes. toro, can add feature service data source map layers using add_feature_server_source function.

title: Add a feature service source
text: time, can simply provide URL feature service toro handle rest. default query parameters appended URL retrieve data GeoJSON format.
code: library(toro)  service_url <- paste0(   "https://services1.arcgis.com/",   "VwarAUbcaX64Jhub/arcgis/rest/services/",   "World_Exclusive_Economic_Zones_Boundaries/FeatureServer" )  map() |>   add_feature_server_source(     service_url,     "eez"   ) |>   add_line_layer(id = "eez_lines", source = "eez")

title: Add a feature service source
text: need control query parameters, can provide full query URL directly source_url argument set append_query_url empty string prevent appending default query parameters.
code: library(toro)  # We supply the full query URL here, so we set append_query_url to an empty string to # avoid appending the default query parameters. service_url <- paste0(   "https://services1.arcgis.com/",   "AYGZtmUtpARUKBlB/arcgis/rest/services/",   "Te_Reo_M%C4%81ori_Place_Names/FeatureServer/",   "4/query?where=1=1&outFields=*&f=geojson" )  map() |>   add_feature_server_source(     service_url,     "maori_awa_data",     append_query_url = ""   ) |>   add_line_layer(     id = "awa_lines",     source = "maori_awa_data",     hover_column = "name_mi",     popup_column = "name"   )

title: Add a GeoJSON/sf source
text: GeoJSON source format used represent geographical features (well non-geographic attributes). sources can hold features like polygons, lines, points, circles, . R GeoJSON object contain geojson class. sf object simple feature data frame contains geometry column along columns containing variety attributes. R sf object contain sf class. example, use quakes dataset comes base R. quakes , default, just data frame. use sf package turn simple feature data frame. point data still GeoJSON data (sf object), however, add_source() function contains ability turn sf object geojson object. means can pass quakes_data add_source() internally converted sf geojson. purpose example, split quakes_data two objects: sf object containing quakes magnitude 6 geojson object containing quakes magnitude 6
code: library(sf) #> Linking to GEOS 3.13.0, GDAL 3.8.5, PROJ 9.5.1; sf_use_s2() is TRUE library(geojsonsf) library(dplyr) #>  #> Attaching package: 'dplyr' #> The following objects are masked from 'package:stats': #>  #>     filter, lag #> The following objects are masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union library(toro)  data(quakes) quakes_data <- quakes |>   dplyr::mutate(hover = paste0("Magnitude: ", mag, "</br>Depth: ", depth)) |>   sf::st_as_sf(coords = c("long", "lat"), crs = 4326) # Keep as a sf object sf_quakes_data <- quakes_data |>   dplyr::filter(mag < 6) print(sf_quakes_data) #> Simple feature collection with 995 features and 4 fields #> Geometry type: POINT #> Dimension:     XY #> Bounding box:  xmin: 165.67 ymin: -38.59 xmax: 188.13 ymax: -10.72 #> Geodetic CRS:  WGS 84 #> First 10 features: #>    depth mag stations                         hover              geometry #> 1    562 4.8       41 Magnitude: 4.8</br>Depth: 562 POINT (181.62 -20.42) #> 2    650 4.2       15 Magnitude: 4.2</br>Depth: 650 POINT (181.03 -20.62) #> 3     42 5.4       43  Magnitude: 5.4</br>Depth: 42     POINT (184.1 -26) #> 4    626 4.1       19 Magnitude: 4.1</br>Depth: 626 POINT (181.66 -17.97) #> 5    649 4.0       11   Magnitude: 4</br>Depth: 649 POINT (181.96 -20.42) #> 6    195 4.0       12   Magnitude: 4</br>Depth: 195 POINT (184.31 -19.68) #> 7     82 4.8       43  Magnitude: 4.8</br>Depth: 82   POINT (166.1 -11.7) #> 8    194 4.4       15 Magnitude: 4.4</br>Depth: 194 POINT (181.93 -28.11) #> 9    211 4.7       35 Magnitude: 4.7</br>Depth: 211 POINT (181.74 -28.74) #> 10   622 4.3       19 Magnitude: 4.3</br>Depth: 622 POINT (179.59 -17.47)  # Transform into a geojson object geojson_quakes_data <- quakes_data |>   dplyr::filter(mag >= 6) |>   geojsonsf::sf_geojson() print(geojson_quakes_data) #> {"type":"FeatureCollection","features":[{"type":"Feature","properties":{"depth":139,"mag":6.1,"stations":94,"hover":"Magnitude: 6.1</br>Depth: 139"},"geometry":{"type":"Point","coordinates":[169.92,-20.7]}},{"type":"Feature","properties":{"depth":50,"mag":6.0,"stations":83,"hover":"Magnitude: 6</br>Depth: 50"},"geometry":{"type":"Point","coordinates":[165.96,-13.64]}},{"type":"Feature","properties":{"depth":127,"mag":6.4,"stations":122,"hover":"Magnitude: 6.4</br>Depth: 127"},"geometry":{"type":"Point","coordinates":[167.62,-15.56]}},{"type":"Feature","properties":{"depth":242,"mag":6.0,"stations":132,"hover":"Magnitude: 6</br>Depth: 242"},"geometry":{"type":"Point","coordinates":[167.02,-12.23]}},{"type":"Feature","properties":{"depth":165,"mag":6.0,"stations":119,"hover":"Magnitude: 6</br>Depth: 165"},"geometry":{"type":"Point","coordinates":[170.56,-21.59]}}]} #> $... #> NULL

title: Add a GeoJSON/sf source
text: two ways add GeoJSON/sf source map:

title: Add a GeoJSON/sf source
text: Using add_source() function, can add GeoJSON source passing sf geojson object directly data argument. sf objects automatically converted GeoJSON format added source. can use source adding layers map specifying source argument layer functions. can pass either mapor mapProxy object add_source().
code: map() |>   set_bounds(bounds = quakes_data) |>   add_source("sf_quakes_data", data = sf_quakes_data) |>   add_source("geojson_quakes_data", data = geojson_quakes_data) |>   add_circle_layer(     id = "quakes_circles_sf",     source = "sf_quakes_data",     hover_column = "hover",     paint = get_paint_options("circle", list(colour = "orange"))   ) |>     add_circle_layer(     id = "quakes_circles_geojson",     source = "geojson_quakes_data",     hover_column = "hover",     paint = get_paint_options("circle", list(colour = "red"))   )

what: Adding the source directly in the layer function
title: Add a GeoJSON/sf source
text: Alternatively, can add GeoJSON/sf source directly within layer function passing sf object GeoJSON string source argument. case source ID automatically generated appending layer ID source-. example, add layer id = "my_layer" source sf object, source added map ID "source-my_layer". allows add source layer single step, without needing call add_source() separately.
code: map() |>   set_bounds(bounds = quakes_data) |>   add_circle_layer(     id = "quakes_circles_sf",     source = sf_quakes_data,     hover_column = "hover",     paint = get_paint_options("circle", list(colour = "orange"))   ) |>     add_circle_layer(     id = "quakes_circles_geojson",     source = geojson_quakes_data,     hover_column = "hover",     paint = get_paint_options("circle", list(colour = "red"))   )

title: Add an image source
text: two ways add image source map:

title: Add an image source
text: Using add_image() function, can add image source passing image URL file path directly url argument. can pass either mapor mapProxy object add_image().
code: library(toro) library(sf) #> Linking to GEOS 3.13.0, GDAL 3.8.5, PROJ 9.5.1; sf_use_s2() is TRUE  epi_location <- data.frame(   lon = 174.82103783265418,   lat = -41.30961916092329 ) |>   st_as_sf(coords = c("lon", "lat"), crs = 4326)  map() |>   add_image(     image_id = "epi_logo",     image_url = "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Epi-interactive-Ltd/toro/refs/heads/dev/man/figures/epi-logo.png"   ) |>   add_symbol_layer(     id = "epi",     source = epi_location,     layout = get_layout_options(       "symbol",       list(         icon_image = "epi_logo",         icon_size = 0.1       )     )   ) |>   set_bounds(epi_location, max_zoom = 13)

title: Getting started with toro
text: install development version GitHub: installed, package can used R console within Shiny applications.
code: remotes::install_github("Epi-interactive-Ltd/toro")

title: Getting started with toro
text: create default map widget just call map function.
code: map()

title: Getting started with toro
text: passing parameters map function can customise several features map.
code: map(   style = "natgeo",   center = c(174, -41),   zoom = 2 )

title: Getting started with toro
text: can add layers map using layer functions like add_symbol_layer, add_fill_layer, etc. detailed information see layers vignette.
code: map() |>   add_symbol_layer(     id = "marker-layer",     source = sf::st_as_sf(       data.frame(lat = c(-41.306579555227245), long = c(174.82263228933076)),       coords = c("long", "lat"),       crs = 4326     )   )

title: Getting started with toro
text: highly recommended read layers vignette next.

title: Clusters/Spiderfying
text: Note: Clusters currently work symbol(icon/pin) layers. Clusters disabled default, symbols coordinates always produce cluster (even can_cluster set false). Clicking cluster one two things: Zoom clusters pins Spiderfy pins cluster become visible
code: base_map  |>   add_symbol_layer(     id = "earthquakes",     source = quakes_data,     layout = toro::get_layout_options(       "symbol",       options = list(         icon_image = "map_pin",         icon_size = 0.05       )     ),     popup_column = "popup"   )

title: Clusters/Spiderfying
code: base_map  |>   add_symbol_layer(     id = "earthquakes",     source = quakes_data,     layout = toro::get_layout_options(       "symbol",       options = list(         icon_image = "map_pin",         icon_size = 0.05       )     ),     popup_column = "popup",     can_cluster = TRUE   )

title: Clusters/Spiderfying
text: can customise cluster appearance using clusterOptions. can set globally creating map specific layers adding layer.

title: Clusters/Spiderfying
text: clusterOptions.circleOptions changes appearance clusters circle. Available circle (paint/layout) properties can found .
code: map(   imageSources = list(     list(       id = "map_pin",       url = "https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/f/f2/678111-map-marker-512.png"     )   ),   # Setting the appearance globally for all clusters in the map   clusterOptions = list(     circleOptions = list(       paint = list(         "circle-color" = "#ebc642",         "circle-stroke-color" = "#fc038c",         "circle-radius" = 50       )     )   ) ) |>   add_symbol_layer(     id = "earthquakes",     source = quakes_data |> head(nrow(quakes_data) / 2),     layout = toro::get_layout_options(       "symbol",       options = list(         icon_image = "map_pin",         icon_size = 0.05       )     ),     popup_column = "popup",     can_cluster = TRUE   ) |>   add_symbol_layer(     id = "earthquakes_two",     source = quakes_data |> tail(nrow(quakes_data) / 2),     layout = toro::get_layout_options(       "symbol",       options = list(         icon_image = "map_pin",         icon_size = 0.05       )     ),     popup_column = "popup",     can_cluster = TRUE,     # Override the global cluster appearance for this layer only     clusterOptions = list(       circleOptions = list(         paint = list(           "circle-color" = "green",           "circle-stroke-color" = "orange",           "circle-radius" = 20         )       )     )   )

title: Clusters/Spiderfying
text: clusterOptions.textOptions changes appearance clusters text. Available text(symbol) (paint/layout) properties can found .
code: base_map  |>   add_symbol_layer(     id = "earthquakes",     source = quakes_data,     layout = toro::get_layout_options(       "symbol",       options = list(         icon_image = "map_pin",         icon_size = 0.05       )     ),     popup_column = "popup",     can_cluster = TRUE,     clusterOptions = list(       textOptions = list(         paint = list(           "text-color" = "red"         )       )     )   )

title: Clusters/Spiderfying
text: example advanced customising (see example).
code: base_map |>   add_symbol_layer(     id = "earthquakes",     source = quakes_data,     layout = toro::get_layout_options(       "symbol",       options = list(         icon_image = "map_pin",         icon_size = 0.05       )     ),     popup_column = "popup",     can_cluster = TRUE,     clusterOptions = list(       circleOptions = list(         paint = list(           "circle-color" = get_column_steps(             "point_count",             c(2, 5, 10),             c("red", "orange", "yellow", "green")           ),           "circle-stroke-color" = get_column_steps(             "point_count",             c(2, 5, 10),             c("red", "orange", "yellow", "green")           ),           "circle-radius" = get_column_steps(             "point_count",             c(2, 5, 10),             c(10, 20, 30, 40)           )         ),         layout = list(           "circle-sort-key" = get_column_steps(             "point_count",             c(2, 5, 10),             c(4, 3, 2, 1)           )         )       )     )   )

title: Clusters/Spiderfying
text: Spiderfying handles viewing multiple pins cluster (pins close proximity). map zoomed max zoom level cluster still appear spiderfy clicked . user able view pins even appear cluster. two reasons cluster need spiderfy: maps max zoom still pins close enough coordinates cluster (layer clustering enabled). group pins share coordinates. case, regardless whether layer clustering enabled cluster always appear. due fact without way user know multiple pins stacked top look like single pin.

title: Clusters/Spiderfying
text: setting low max zoom can see points clusters points know coordinates (examples) close enough proximity max zoom still cluster.
code: map(   maxZoom = 5,   imageSources = list(     list(       id = "map_pin",       url = "https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/f/f2/678111-map-marker-512.png"     )   ) ) |>   add_symbol_layer(     id = "earthquakes",     source = quakes_data,     layout = toro::get_layout_options(       "symbol",       options = list(         icon_image = "map_pin",         icon_size = 0.05       )     ),     popup_column = "popup",     can_cluster = TRUE   )

title: Clusters/Spiderfying
text: pins coordinates always appear cluster, even layer set can_cluster false.
code: base_map |>   add_symbol_layer(     id = "duplicated_pins",     source = duplicated_data,     layout = toro::get_layout_options(       "symbol",       options = list(         icon_image = "map_pin",         icon_size = 0.05       )     ),     popup_column = "popup",     can_cluster = FALSE   )

title: Clusters/Spiderfying
text: 2 aspects spiderfying customisable:

what: Customise max pins shown in spiderfy
title: Clusters/Spiderfying
text: default spiderfying shows 100 pins. can see example one clusters says 501 points can see 100 pins spiderfying. can changed setting clusterOptions.spiderfyOptions.maxSpiderfyPins creating map. , want override global setting specific layer, can set clusterOptions.spiderfyOptions.maxSpiderfyPins adding layer.
code: map(   imageSources = list(     list(       id = "map_pin",       url = "https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/f/f2/678111-map-marker-512.png"     )   ),   clusterOptions = list(     spiderfyOptions = list(       maxSpiderfyPins = 500     )   ) ) |>   add_symbol_layer(     id = "duplicated_pins",     source = duplicated_data,     layout = toro::get_layout_options(       "symbol",       options = list(         icon_image = "map_pin",         icon_size = 0.05       )     ),     popup_column = "popup",     can_cluster = FALSE,     clusterOptions = list(       spiderfyOptions = list(         # maxSpiderfyPins = 5 # This would override the global setting for this layer only       )     )   )

title: Clusters/Spiderfying
text: Like clusters can customise spiderfys appearance changing clusterOptions.spiderfyOptions.maxSpiderfyPins. Spiderfying appearance can set globally, per layer. Available line (paint/layout) properties can found .
code: map(   imageSources = list(     list(       id = "map_pin",       url = "https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/f/f2/678111-map-marker-512.png"     )   ),   clusterOptions = list(     spiderfyOptions = list(       paint = list(         "line-color" = "purple",         "line-width" = 2       )     )   ) ) |>   add_symbol_layer(     id = "duplicated_pins",     source = duplicated_data,     layout = toro::get_layout_options(       "symbol",       options = list(         icon_image = "map_pin",         icon_size = 0.05       )     ),     popup_column = "popup",     can_cluster = FALSE   )

title: Map tiles
text: can use set_tile_layer() function update tiles map options defined loadedTiles map created.
code: library(shiny) library(toro)   ui <- fluidPage(   toro::mapOutput("map"),   selectInput(     "tiles",     "Tiles",     choices = toro::get_tile_options(),     selected = "satellite"   ), )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$map <- toro::renderMap({     toro::map(style = "satellite", loadedTiles = toro::get_tile_options())   })    observe({     req(input$tiles)     toro::mapProxy("map") |>       set_tile_layer(input$tiles)   }) }  if (interactive()) {   shinyApp(ui, server) }

title: Map tiles
text: can also change maps tiles via tile controller. tile controller must placed inside control panel. information adding controls map see controls vignette.
code: library(shiny) library(toro)  all_tiles <- toro::get_tile_options()  ui <- fluidPage(   toro::mapOutput("map") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$map <- toro::renderMap({     toro::map(style = "satellite", loadedTiles = all_tiles) |>       add_control_panel(         panel_id = "demo-panel"       ) |>       add_tile_selector_control(         available_tiles = all_tiles,         panel_id = "demo-panel"       )   })  }  if (interactive()) {   shinyApp(ui, server) }

title: Integration with Shiny
text: modify map shiny page without re-rendering whole widget can use mapProxy. Typically use map create initial static map widget, use mapProxy update dynamic aspects map.
code: library(shiny) library(toro)   ui <- fluidPage(   toro::mapOutput("map"),   selectInput(     "tiles",     "Tiles",     choices = toro::get_tile_options(),     selected = "satellite"   ), )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$map <- toro::renderMap({     toro::map(style = "satellite", loadedTiles = toro::get_tile_options())   })    observe({     req(input$tiles)     toro::mapProxy("map") |>       set_tile_layer(input$tiles)   }) }  if (interactive()) {   shinyApp(ui, server) }

title: Integration with Shiny
text: Shiny modify parts map need provide ID aspect want update.

title: Integration with Shiny
text: ID map shiny context. ID use render map also modify using mapProxy. map ID also used events status sections. renderMap() mapOutput() mapProxy()

title: Integration with Shiny
text: add layer map can provide ID layer. ID can used modify layer later . Add functions: add_layer() add_fill_layer() add_circle_layer() add_line_layer() add_symbol_layer() add_text_layer() add_lat_lng_grid() Visibility functions: show_layer() hide_layer() toggle_lat_lng_grid() toggle_clustering() Controls target layers: add_cluster_toggle() add_visibility_toggle() remove_cluster_toggle() remove_visibility_toggle() Layer customisation: set_paint_property() set_layout_property()

title: Integration with Shiny
text: add source map can provide ID source. ID can used modify source later . add source inside add layer function source ID auto generated . Add functions: add_source() add_feature_server_source() add_image() Modifying functions: set_source_data()

title: Integration with Shiny
text: add control map can provide ID control. ID can used modify control later . controls linked element IDs (like cluster visibility toggles needing layer IDs). Add functions: add_custom_control() add_cursor_coords_control() add_control_panel() add_control_group() add_draw_control() Remove functions: remove_control() remove_custom_control() remove_cursor_coords_control() remove_control_group() remove_draw_control()

title: Integration with Shiny
text: add route map can provide ID route. ID can used modify route later . Add functions: add_route() Remove functions: remove_route() functions: play_route() pause_route()

title: Integration with Shiny
text: using draw control draw shapes. shape ID can used delete shape delete_drawn_shape().

title: Integration with Shiny
text: default map initially loads trigger loaded event Shiny. can use event trigger actions Shiny app. Shiny input value available input$<mapId>_loaded, <mapId> output ID map.
code: # In UI mapOutput("my_map")  # In server loaded events watching <map_id>_loaded input values observe({   req(input$my_map_loaded)   print("Map has loaded!") }) |>   bindEvent(input$my_map_loaded)

title: Integration with Shiny
text: Anytime feature (polygon, line, point) clicked map, feature_click event fired. can listen event using observer Shiny app. Just append _feature_click map ID get input ID (input$<mapId>_feature_click).
code: # In UI mapOutput("my_map")  # In server feature click events watching <map_id>_feature_click input values observe({   print(get_clicked_feature(input$my_map_feature_click)) })

title: Integration with Shiny
text: Whenever map view changes (zoom/bounds) bounds / zoom event fired. can listen event using observer Shiny app. Just append _bounds / _zoom map ID get input ID (input$<mapId>_bounds / input$<mapId>_zoom).
code: # In UI mapOutput("my_map")  # In server bounds / zoom events watching <map_id>_bounds / <map_id>_zoom input values observe({   print(input$my_map_bounds) })  observe({   print(input$my_map_zoom) })

what: Interactive & Customisable Maps using the MapLibre GL JS Library
title: Interactive & Customisable Maps using the MapLibre GL JS Library
text: Create interactive maps can keep complex visualisations large datasets, useful interface MapLibre GL JS library. Users can create maps directly console, HTML widget within ‘Shiny’ web applications, render spatial data quickly many customisable options (clusters, custom icons, map layers, backgrounds). goal package make easier interpret explore large spatial datasets within context ‘Shiny’ dashboard, without long loading times waiting map update new data.

title: Interactive & Customisable Maps using the MapLibre GL JS Library
text: Install CRAN: , install development version GitHub:
code: install.packages("toro") # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("Epi-interactive-Ltd/toro")

what: Add animation controls to a toro map — add_animation_controls
title: Add animation controls to a toro map — add_animation_controls
text: Adds play/pause/stop buttons control route animations map. Optionally includes speed control slider adjusting animation speed.

title: Add animation controls to a toro map — add_animation_controls
code: add_animation_controls(   map,   route_id = NULL,   position = "top-right",   panel_id = NULL,   buttons = c("play", "pause"),   include_speed_control = FALSE,   speed_values = c(0.5, 1, 2),   speed_labels = c("Slow", "Normal", "Fast"),   settings = list() )

title: Add animation controls to a toro map — add_animation_controls
text: map toro map object map proxy object. route_id Optional route ID control. NULL, controls routes. position Position controls map. Default "top-right". panel_id Optional control panel ID add controls instead map. buttons Character vector buttons include. Options: "play", "pause", "stop". Default c("play", "pause"). include_speed_control Logical. Whether include speed control slider. Default FALSE. speed_values Numeric vector speed values speed slider. Default c(0.5, 1, 2) slow, normal, fast speeds. speed_labels Character vector labels speed values. Default c("Slow", "Normal", "Fast"). settings list additional settings controls.

title: Add animation controls to a toro map — add_animation_controls
text: map map proxy object chaining.

title: Add animation controls to a toro map — add_animation_controls

what: Add a circle layer to a map or map proxy — add_circle_layer
title: Add a circle layer to a map or map proxy — add_circle_layer
text: Add circle layer map map proxy

title: Add a circle layer to a map or map proxy — add_circle_layer
code: add_circle_layer(   map,   id,   source,   paint = NULL,   layout = NULL,   popup_column = NULL,   hover_column = NULL,   can_cluster = FALSE,   under_id = NULL,   filter = NULL,   ... )

title: Add a circle layer to a map or map proxy — add_circle_layer
text: map map object map proxy layer added. id unique identifier layer. source data source layer, GeoJSON, converted. paint list paint options styling layer. See get_paint_options() defaults options. layout list layout options layer. See get_layout_options() defaults options. popup_column column name use popups. Default NULL. hover_column column name use hover effects. Default NULL. can_cluster Whether layer can clustered. Default FALSE. under_id ID layer already map place layer . Default NULL. filter filter expression apply layer. Default NULL. See get_layer_filter() details create filter expressions. ... Additional arguments include layer definition. clusterOptions: list options clustering, can_cluster TRUE. See cluster vignette details available options.

title: Add a circle layer to a map or map proxy — add_circle_layer
text: updated map object circle layer added.

what: Add a cluster toggle control to the map or control panel — add_cluster_toggle
title: Add a cluster toggle control to the map or control panel — add_cluster_toggle
text: Creates toggle button can enable/disable clustering specific layer.

title: Add a cluster toggle control to the map or control panel — add_cluster_toggle
code: add_cluster_toggle(   map,   layer_id,   control_id = NULL,   left_label = "Toggle Clustering",   right_label = NULL,   initial_state = FALSE,   position = "top-right",   panel_id = NULL,   section_title = NULL,   group_id = NULL )

title: Add a cluster toggle control to the map or control panel — add_cluster_toggle
text: map map map proxy object. layer_id ID layer toggle clustering . control_id ID control. NULL, defaults "cluster-toggle-<layer_id>". left_label Label text toggle button. Default "Toggle Clustering". right_label Label text toggle button clustering . Default "Clustering ". initial_state Initial clustering state. Default FALSE. position Position map using control panel. Default "top-right". panel_id ID control panel add (optional). section_title Section title added control panel. group_id ID control group add (optional).

title: Add a cluster toggle control to the map or control panel — add_cluster_toggle
text: map map proxy object chaining.

what: Add a draw control to the map — add_draw_control
title: Add a draw control to the map — add_draw_control
text: draw control allows users draw shapes (polygons, lines, points) map. drawn shapes can styled managed control options. Information drawn shapes can retrieved Shiny using input$map_shape_created (map ID map) reactive value.

title: Add a draw control to the map — add_draw_control
code: add_draw_control(   map,   id = "draw_control",   position = "top-right",   modes = c("polygon"),   active_colour = "#04AAC1",   inactive_colour = "#04AAC1",   mode_labels = list(),   panel_id = NULL,   section_title = NULL,   group_id = NULL )

title: Add a draw control to the map — add_draw_control
text: map map map proxy object. id ID draw control. position position draw control map. Default "top-right". Options "top-left", "top-right", "bottom-left", "bottom-right". modes vector modes enable draw control. Default c("polygon"). Options include "polygon", "delete", "line", "point". active_colour colour drawn shapes. Default "#04AAC1". inactive_colour colour inactive shapes. Default "#04AAC1". mode_labels named list labels mode. example, list(polygon = "Draw Polygon", delete = "Delete Shape"). panel_id ID control panel add (optional). section_title Section title added control panel. group_id Optional group ID grouping controls within panel.

title: Add a draw control to the map — add_draw_control
text: map map proxy object chaining.

title: Add a fill layer to a map or map proxy — add_fill_layer
text: map map object map proxy layer added. id unique identifier layer. source data source layer, GeoJSON, converted. paint list paint options styling layer. See get_paint_options() defaults options. layout list layout options layer. See get_layout_options() defaults options. popup_column column name use popups. Default NULL. hover_column column name use hover effects. Default NULL. can_cluster Whether layer can clustered. Default FALSE. under_id ID layer already map place layer . Default NULL. filter filter expression apply layer. Default NULL. See get_layer_filter() details create filter expressions. ... Additional arguments include layer definition. clusterOptions: list options clustering, can_cluster TRUE. See cluster vignette details available options.

title: Add a layer to a map or map proxy — add_layer
code: add_layer(   map,   id,   type = "fill",   source,   paint = NULL,   layout = NULL,   popup_column = NULL,   hover_column = NULL,   can_cluster = FALSE,   under_id = NULL,   filter = NULL,   ... )

title: Add a layer to a map or map proxy — add_layer
text: map map object map proxy layer added. id unique identifier layer. type type layer add (e.g., "fill", "circle", "line"). Default "fill". source data source layer, GeoJSON, converted. paint list paint options styling layer. See get_paint_options() defaults options. layout list layout options layer. See get_layout_options() defaults options. popup_column column name use popups. Default NULL. hover_column column name use hover effects. Default NULL. can_cluster Whether layer can clustered. Default FALSE. under_id ID layer already map place layer . Default NULL. filter filter expression apply layer. Default NULL. See get_layer_filter() details create filter expressions. ... Additional arguments include layer definition. clusterOptions: list options clustering, can_cluster TRUE. See cluster vignette details available options.

title: Add a layer to a map or map proxy — add_layer
text: updated map object new layer added.

title: Add a layer to a map or map proxy — add_layer
text: source string referring existing source, converted GeoJSON source added map automatically. Id source generated appending layer Id source-. example, add layer id = "my_layer" source sf object, source added map ID "source-my_layer".

title: Add a layer to a map or map proxy — add_layer

what: Add a layer selector control to the map or control panel — add_layer_selector_control
title: Add a layer selector control to the map or control panel — add_layer_selector_control
text: Creates drop-selector allows switching layers, showing selected layer hiding others. useful comparing different data layers allowing users choose mutually exclusive visualizations.

title: Add a layer selector control to the map or control panel — add_layer_selector_control
code: add_layer_selector_control(   map,   layer_ids,   labels = NULL,   default_layer = NULL,   none_option = FALSE,   none_label = "None",   position = "top-right",   panel_id = NULL,   section_title = NULL,   group_id = NULL )

title: Add a layer selector control to the map or control panel — add_layer_selector_control
text: map map map proxy object. layer_ids Vector layer IDs include selector. labels Named vector labels layers. NULL, uses layer IDs directly. default_layer Default layer select. NULL, uses first layer. none_option Whether include "None" option hides layers. Default FALSE. none_label Label "None" option. Default "None". position Position map using control panel. Default "top-right". panel_id ID control panel add (optional). section_title Section title added control panel. group_id ID control group add (optional).

title: Add a route to a toro map which can be animated — add_route
code: if(interactive()){ library(shiny) library(toro) library(sf)  line_data <- sf::st_sf(   id = 1,   geometry = sf::st_sfc(     sf::st_linestring(       cbind(c(172.2041, 163.9383), c(-32.56960, -46.43999))     ),     crs = 4326   ) )  ui <- fluidPage(  tagList(    mapOutput("map"),    actionButton("play_route", "Play Route Animation"),    actionButton("pause_route", "Pause Route Animation"),    actionButton("remove_route", "Remove Route")  ) ) server <- function(input, output, session) {  output$map <- renderMap({    map() |>      add_route(route_id = "route_line", points = line_data)  })   observe({    req(input$map_loaded)    mapProxy("map") |>      play_route(route_id = "route_line")  }) |>    bindEvent(input$play_route)    observe({    req(input$map_loaded)    mapProxy("map") |>      pause_route(route_id = "route_line")  }) |>    bindEvent(input$pause_route)    observe({    req(input$map_loaded)    mapProxy("map") |>      remove_route(route_id = "route_line")  }) |>    bindEvent(input$remove_route) } }

what: Add a source to the map — add_source
title: Add a source to the map — add_source
text: Add source map

title: Add a source to the map — add_source
code: add_source(map, source_id, data, type = "geojson", cluster = FALSE, ...)

title: Add a source to the map — add_source
text: map map map proxy object. source_id ID source. data data source, typically GeoJSON format. type type source. Default "geojson". options include "vector" "raster". cluster Whether enable clustering source. Default FALSE. ... Additional arguments pass directly JS addSource function. Documentation can found MapLibre GL JS docs. id: Alternative source_id backward compatibility. source_id id provided, source_id take precedence.

title: Add a source to the map — add_source
text: map map proxy object chaining.

title: Add a source to the map — add_source
text: add source directly add layer function, source ID automatically generated source-{layer-id}.

what: Add a speed control to the map or control panel — add_speed_control
title: Add a speed control to the map or control panel — add_speed_control
text: speed control allows users adjust speed animation map, time-based animation controlled timeline control. can added standalone control map within control panel better organization multiple controls.

title: Add a speed control to the map or control panel — add_speed_control
code: add_speed_control(   map,   values = c(0.5, 1, 2),   labels = c("Slow", "Normal", "Fast"),   default_index = 2,   position = "top-right",   panel_id = NULL,   section_title = NULL,   group_id = NULL )

title: Add a speed control to the map or control panel — add_speed_control
text: map map map proxy object. values Vector speed multiplier values. Default c(0.5, 1, 2). labels Vector labels speed value. Default c("Slow", "Normal", "Fast"). default_index Index default speed (1-based). Default 2. position Position map using control panel. Default "top-right". panel_id ID control panel add (optional). section_title Section title added control panel. group_id Optional ID group add control within panel.

title: Add a speed control to the map or control panel — add_speed_control
text: map map proxy object chaining.

Default NULL. hover_column column name use hover effects. Default NULL. can_cluster Whether layer can clustered. Default FALSE. under_id ID layer already map place layer . Default NULL. filter filter expression apply layer. Default NULL. See get_layer_filter() details create filter expressions. ... Additional arguments include layer definition. clusterOptions: list options clustering, can_cluster TRUE. See cluster vignette details available options.

title: Delete a drawn shape from the map — delete_drawn_shape
text: proxy map proxy object created mapProxy(). shape_id ID shape delete.

title: Delete a drawn shape from the map — delete_drawn_shape
text: map proxy object chaining.

title: Delete a drawn shape from the map — delete_drawn_shape
code: if(interactive()){ library(shiny) library(toro)  ui <- fluidPage(  tagList(    mapOutput("map"),    selectInput("shape_ids", "Drawn shape IDs", choices = NULL),    actionButton("remove_drawn_shape", "Remove drawn shape")  ) ) server <- function(input, output, session) {  drawn_shape_ids <- reactiveVal(character())   output$map <- renderMap({    map() |>     add_draw_control()  })   # Update the select input options with current shape IDs  observe({    req(input$map_loaded)    updateSelectInput(inputId = "shape_ids", choices = drawn_shape_ids())  })   # Update the list of drawn shape IDs when a new shape is created  observe({    req(input$map_loaded, input$map_shape_created)    new_shape <- get_drawn_shape(input$map_shape_created)    drawn_shape_ids(c(drawn_shape_ids(), new_shape$id))  }) |>    bindEvent(input$map_shape_created)   # Delete the selected drawn shape when the button is clicked  observe({    req(input$map_loaded, input$shape_ids)    mapProxy("map") |>      delete_drawn_shape(input$shape_ids)    # Remove the deleted shape ID from the list of drawn shape IDs    drawn_shape_ids(setdiff(drawn_shape_ids(), input$shape_ids))  }) |>    bindEvent(input$remove_drawn_shape) } }

code: # \donttest{ # Load library library(sf)  data <- data.frame(lon = 174.8210, lat = -41.3096) |>   sf::st_as_sf(coords = c("lon", "lat"), crs = 4326) # Create and export a map my_map <- map() |>   add_circle_layer("epi_circle", source = data)  export_map_image(my_map, file.path(tempdir(), "my_map.png"), width = 1200, height = 800) #> file:////private/var/folders/60/t3r81yp572q7bdlk6hg3rc6w0000gn/T/Rtmp5ZrbGt/file75af51d5200e.html screenshot completed #> Map exported to: /var/folders/60/t3r81yp572q7bdlk6hg3rc6w0000gn/T//Rtmp5ZrbGt/my_map.png # }

code: if(interactive()){ library(shiny) library(spData) library(sf) library(toro)  nz_data <- spData::nz_height |>   sf::st_transform(4326)  ui <- fluidPage(  tagList(    mapOutput("map")  ) ) server <- function(input, output, session) {  output$map <- renderMap({    map() |>      set_bounds(bounds = nz_data) |>      add_circle_layer(        id = "nz_elevation",        source = nz_data      )  })   # Print the clicked feature as an sf object  observe({    req(input$map_loaded, input$map_feature_click)    print(get_clicked_feature(input$map_feature_click))  }) |>    bindEvent(input$map_feature_click) } }

what: Get layout options for a specific layer type — get_layout_options
title: Get layout options for a specific layer type — get_layout_options
text: function returns list layout options based layer type additional options provided.

title: Get layout options for a specific layer type — get_layout_options
code: get_layout_options(layer_type, options = list())

title: Get layout options for a specific layer type — get_layout_options
text: layer_type string indicating type layer (e.g., "fill", "circle", "line"). options list additional options customize layout properties. See MapLibre docs options. line_cap: list options line cap, layer_type "line". Default "round". line_join: list options line join, layer_type "line". Default "round". icon_image: name icon image use symbol layers, layer_type "symbol". Default "toro-pin". icon_size: size icon symbol layers, layer_type "symbol". Default 1. icon_anchor: anchor point icon symbol layers, layer_type "symbol". Default "bottom". text_anchor: anchor point text symbol layers, layer_type "symbol". Default "center". icon_offset: offset icon symbol layers, layer_type "symbol". Default list(0, 0). icon_allow_overlap: Whether allow icons overlap symbol layers, layer_type "symbol". Default TRUE. text_allow_overlap: Whether allow text overlap symbol layers, layer_type "symbol". Default TRUE icon_rotate: rotation angle icons symbol layers, layer_type "symbol". Default 0. icon_flip_horizontal: Whether flip icons horizontally symbol layers, layer_type "symbol". Default FALSE. Note uses icon-flip-horizontal property MapLibre, may supported icons. text_font: font text symbol layers, layer_type "symbol". Default "Open Sans Regular". text_field: text field symbol layers, layer_type "symbol". Default NULL, means text shown. can set column value using get_column() functions show text data. text_size: size text symbol layers, layer_type "symbol". Default 12. can also provide layout options found MapLibre docs specific layer type, included returned list.

title: Get layout options for a specific layer type — get_layout_options
text: list layout options suitable specified layer type.

title: Get layout options for a specific layer type — get_layout_options
text: can provide layout options found MapLibre Layers docs options argument, included returned list. default options just starting point can overridden providing options argument.

title: Get layout options for a specific layer type — get_layout_options
code: get_layout_options("line", list(line_cap = "butt", line_join = "bevel")) #> $`line-cap` #> [1] "butt" #>  #> $`line-join` #> [1] "bevel" #>   get_layout_options("symbol", list(icon_image = "yellow_pin", icon_size = 1.5)) #> $`icon-allow-overlap` #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $`icon-image` #> [1] "yellow_pin" #>  #> $`icon-size` #> [1] 1.5 #>  #> $`icon-anchor` #> [1] "bottom" #>  #> $`icon-offset` #> $`icon-offset`[[1]] #> [1] 0 #>  #> $`icon-offset`[[2]] #> [1] 0 #>  #>  #> $`icon-rotate` #> [1] 0 #>  #> $`text-font` #> $`text-font`[[1]] #> [1] "Open Sans Regular" #>  #>  #> $`text-size` #> [1] 12 #>   # For horizontal flipping, provide left/right versions of your icon or use rotation fallback get_layout_options("symbol", list(icon_image = "arrow", icon_flip_horizontal = TRUE)) #> $`icon-allow-overlap` #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $`icon-image` #> [1] "arrow" #>  #> $`icon-size` #> [1] 1 #>  #> $`icon-anchor` #> [1] "bottom" #>  #> $`icon-offset` #> $`icon-offset`[[1]] #> [1] 0 #>  #> $`icon-offset`[[2]] #> [1] 0 #>  #>  #> $`icon-rotate` #> [1] 0 #>  #> $`text-font` #> $`text-font`[[1]] #> [1] "Open Sans Regular" #>  #>  #> $`text-size` #> [1] 12 #>  #> $`icon-flip-horizontal` #> [1] TRUE #>   # Provide options outside of the defaults get_layout_options(  "circle",  list(    "circle-sort-key" = get_column_steps(      "elevation",      c(3000),      c(100, 200)    )  ) ) #> $`circle-sort-key` #> $`circle-sort-key`[[1]] #> [1] "step" #>  #> $`circle-sort-key`[[2]] #> $`circle-sort-key`[[2]][[1]] #> [1] "get" #>  #> $`circle-sort-key`[[2]][[2]] #> [1] "elevation" #>  #>  #> $`circle-sort-key`[[3]] #> [1] 100 #>  #> $`circle-sort-key`[[4]] #> [1] 3000 #>  #> $`circle-sort-key`[[5]] #> [1] 200 #>  #>

what: Get paint options for a specific layer type — get_paint_options

title: Get paint options for a specific layer type — get_paint_options
code: get_paint_options(layer_type, options = list())

title: Get paint options for a specific layer type — get_paint_options
text: layer_type string indicating type layer (e.g., "fill", "circle", "line"). options list additional options customize paint properties. See MapLibre docs full options. colour: color use layer. Default "grey". color also accepted alias colour. opacity: opacity use layer. Default 1 (fully opaque). line_width: width lines line layers outline width circle layers. Default 1. line_dash: dash pattern line layers. Default dash (solid line). can provide vector numbers specify dash pattern (e.g., c(2, 4) pattern 2 units , 4 units ). outline_colour: color use outline circle fill layers. Default colour. outline_color also accepted alias outline_colour. outline_opacity: opacity use outline circle fill layers. Default opacity. can also provide paint options found MapLibre docs specific layer type, included returned list.

code: if(interactive()){ library(shiny) library(sf) library(toro)  data(quakes) quakes_data <- sf::st_as_sf(quakes, coords = c("long", "lat"), crs = 4326)  ui <- fluidPage(  tagList(    mapOutput("map"),    actionButton("show_layer", "Show Layer"),    actionButton("hide_layer", "Hide Layer")  ) ) server <- function(input, output, session) {  output$map <- renderMap({    map() |>      add_circle_layer(        id = "quakes",        source = quakes_data      )  })   observe({    mapProxy("map") |>      show_layer(layer_id = "quakes")  }) |>    bindEvent(input$show_layer)   observe({    mapProxy("map") |>      hide_layer(layer_id = "quakes")  }) |>    bindEvent(input$hide_layer) } }

title: Create a MapLibre map widget — map

title: Create a MapLibre map widget — map
text: style style map. Default "lightgrey". center initial center map longitude/latitude pair. Default c(174, -41). zoom initial zoom level map. Default 2. width width widget. Optional. height height widget. Optional. session Shiny session object. Default current session. ... Additional options customize map. minZoom: Minimum zoom level (0-24). Default 2. maxZoom: Maximum zoom level (0-24). Default 18. clusterColour: colour cluster circles. Default "#808080". loadedTiles: character vector tile ids load, named list tile options. Full options: c("natgeo", "satellite", "topo", "terrain", "streets", "shaded", "lightgrey"). Default c("lightgrey", "satellite"). initialTileLayer: tile layer use map first loaded. Default NULL (first layer loadedTiles). backgroundColour: background colour map. Default "#D0CFD4". enable3D: Whether enable 3D dragging/view. Default FALSE. initialLoadedLayers: character vector layer ids loaded initial map spinner hidden. Default NULL. spinnerWhileBusy: Whether show spinner map busy (e.g. loading tiles). Default FALSE. busyLoaderBgColour: background colour busy loader. Default "rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.2)". busyLoaderColour: colour busy loader. Default "white". initialLoaderBgColour: background colour initial loader. Default "white". initialLoaderColour: colour initial loader. Default "black". clusterOptions: list options clustering, can_cluster TRUE. See cluster vignette details available options. attributionPosition: position attribution control. Default "bottom-right".

title: Create a MapLibre map widget — map
text: object class htmlwidget representing map.

title: Create a MapLibre map widget — map

code: if(interactive()){ library(shiny) library(toro)  ui <- fluidPage(  tagList(    mapOutput("map"),    checkboxInput("has_zoom_controls", "Remove Zoom Controls", value = TRUE)  ) ) server <- function(input, output, session) {  output$map <- renderMap({    map() |>      add_zoom_control()  })   observe({    req(input$map_loaded)    if (input$has_zoom_controls == TRUE) {      mapProxy("map") |>        add_zoom_control()    } else {      mapProxy("map") |>        remove_zoom_control()    }  }) |>    bindEvent(input$has_zoom_controls) } }

code: if(interactive()){ library(shiny) library(toro) library(sf)  line_data <- sf::st_sf(   id = 1,   geometry = sf::st_sfc(     sf::st_linestring(       cbind(c(172.2041, 163.9383), c(-32.56960, -46.43999))     ),     crs = 4326   ) )  ui <- fluidPage(  tagList(    mapOutput("map"),    actionButton("play_route", "Play Route Animation"),    actionButton("pause_route", "Pause Route Animation"),    actionButton("remove_route", "Remove Route")  ) ) server <- function(input, output, session) {  output$map <- renderMap({    map() |>      add_route(route_id = "route_line", points = line_data)  })   observe({    req(input$map_loaded)    mapProxy("map") |>      play_route(route_id = "route_line")  }) |>    bindEvent(input$play_route)    observe({    req(input$map_loaded)    mapProxy("map") |>      pause_route(route_id = "route_line")  }) |>    bindEvent(input$pause_route)    observe({    req(input$map_loaded)    mapProxy("map") |>      remove_route(route_id = "route_line")  }) |>    bindEvent(input$remove_route) } }

code: if(interactive()){ library(shiny) library(toro) library(sf)  line_data <- sf::st_sf(   id = 1,   geometry = sf::st_sfc(     sf::st_linestring(       cbind(c(172.2041, 163.9383), c(-32.56960, -46.43999))     ),     crs = 4326   ) )  ui <- fluidPage(  tagList(    mapOutput("map"),    actionButton("play_route", "Play Route Animation"),    actionButton("pause_route", "Pause Route Animation"),    actionButton("remove_route", "Remove Route")  ) ) server <- function(input, output, session) {  output$map <- renderMap({    map() |>      add_route(route_id = "route_line", points = line_data)  })   observe({    req(input$map_loaded)    mapProxy("map") |>      play_route(route_id = "route_line")  }) |>    bindEvent(input$play_route)    observe({    req(input$map_loaded)    mapProxy("map") |>      pause_route(route_id = "route_line")  }) |>    bindEvent(input$pause_route)    observe({    req(input$map_loaded)    mapProxy("map") |>      remove_route(route_id = "route_line")  }) |>    bindEvent(input$remove_route) } }

code: if(interactive()){ library(shiny) library(sf) library(toro)  data(quakes) quakes_data <- sf::st_as_sf(quakes, coords = c("long", "lat"), crs = 4326)  ui <- fluidPage(  tagList(    mapOutput("map"),    actionButton("btn", "Update Source Data")  ) ) server <- function(input, output, session) {  get_random_points <- function(data, n = 10) {    random_rows <- runif(n, min = 1, max = nrow(data))    return(data[random_rows, ])  }   output$map <- renderMap({    map() |>      add_symbol_layer(        id = "quakes",        source = get_random_points(quakes_data)      )    })  observe({    mapProxy("map") |>      set_source_data(        source_id = "source-quakes",        data = get_random_points(quakes_data)      )  }) |>  bindEvent(input$btn) } }

title: toro 0.1.0 (2026-04-28)
text: Added tests

title: toro 0.0.7 (2026-04-28)
text: Fixed adding GeoJSON source directly add layer function working Changed documentation formatting

title: toro 0.0.7 (2026-04-28)
text: Changed MIT license AGPL-3

title: toro 0.0.6 (2026-04-24)
text: Adding image/html export options (export_map_image() save_map_html())

title: toro 0.0.6 (2026-04-24)
text: documentation coverage/clean Fixing general route animation bugs + adding documentation Fixing general draw control bugs + adding documentation Allow pinch--zoom mobile Split symbol text layers two separate functions (add_symbol_layer() add_text_layer()) Adding different class names popups (popup-ovelay) hovers (hover-ovelay) customised styling

title: toro 0.0.5 (2026-03-05)
text: Added cluster/spiderfy customisation (see Cluster/Spiderfying vignette details). loadedTiles now allows supply tile options tile set (see Map Tiles vignette details). Added route animations timeline controls (see Animations vignette details).

title: toro 0.0.5 (2026-03-05)
text: Fixed clusters showing Better clustering around antimeridian line Fixed issues around adding sources add layer functions Updated add_feature_server_source() allow full url passed user can specify query want make feature server Fixed under_id parameter layers can added specific layer already map Fixed popups showing null/undefined Fixed clusters disappearing max zoom Fixed pins coordinates always appear cluster (spiderfy) Fixed attribution closing default Various documentation/vignette improvements updates Changed “light-grey” style “lightgrey”
